oatmeal-cookie.name: Oatmeal Cookie
instructions: In a shot glass, layer butterscotch schnapps, Irish cream and Jagermeister.
Float cinnamon schnapps on top.

oatmeal-peanut-butter-cookies.name: Oatmeal Peanut Butter Cookies
instructions: In a large bowl, cream together 1/2 cup butter or margarine, 1/2 cup peanut butter, white sugar, brown sugar, and vanilla. Add egg and beat well.
In another bowl, combine the flour, baking soda, baking powder, and salt. Add these dry ingredients to the creamed mixture. Stir. Add oatmeal and stir.
Drop by teaspoons onto greased baking sheet, and press each mound down with a fork to form 1/4 inch thick cookies. Bake at 177°C (175 degrees C) for 10 minutes, or until cookies are a light brown.

To Make Filling: Cream 3 tablespoons butter or margarine with the confectioners' sugar, 1/2 cup smooth peanut butter, and the cream. Spread filling onto half of the cooled cookies, then top with the other half to form sandwiches.

oatmeal-raisin-cookie-pancakes.name: Oatmeal Raisin Cookie Pancakes
instructions: Process oats in a food processor until finely ground. Mix oats, flour, baking soda, baking powder, salt, cinnamon, and nutmeg together in a bowl.
Whisk buttermilk, milk, sugar, 1/4 cup vegetable oil, and egg in a separate bowl until smooth; mix into oat mixture until batter is well-mixed, about 30 strokes. Fold raisins and walnuts into batter.

Lightly oil a skillet or griddle and place over medium heat. Drop batter by large spoonfuls onto the griddle and cook until bubbles form and the edges are dry, 3 to 4 minutes. Flip and cook until browned on the other side, 2 to 4 minutes. Repeat with remaining batter.

octopus-confit-argiro.name: Octopus Confit
instructions: For the octopus knot we will use as small a vessel as possible so that the few liquids are concentrated around the octopus tentacles. With the slow roasting, the octopus will melt into the oil.
Put them in the vessel and add all the other materials. Garlic with its dress, pepper beans, hot pepper (optional) laurel, marinated in slices, tomatoes, oregano or some thyme.
Pour with vinegar and olive oil until the octopus is covered.
Cover with non-stick paper and seal the vessel with aluminum foil or lid to keep all the liquids inside.
Bake at 170oC on the bottom grill for 45' until the octopus softens, but be careful not to melt.
We leave it covered in its zooms to cool.
Yet we prepare the fava.

octopus-with-teriyaki-sauce-on-lentil-fa.name: Octopus With Teriyaki Sauce (on Lentil Fava Bed)
instructions: 1. Goodly washed all the cookware with shredded rounds, on top of boiling water in steamed cooking pot, 2-2.
2. Cook in steam up to 5 hours.
3. Wait for the oda room temperature after softening well.
4. On the other hand, start preparing lentilmek fava.
5. Goodly washed into the yellow lentilm 1 onions, 2 teeth garlic, lemon juice, sugar and salt, add water until a finger on the top and slowly boil.
6. Goodly pull well with a robot when it becomes softening and lentilizing, unload and leave it to be stacked.
7. Cut the floor into thick slices, roast up to 5 minutes with olive oil and teriyaki sauce in a fried teflon pan.
8. Then serve with mountain cake on the bed from lentilmek fava.

old-fashioned-cushaw-pie.name: Old-fashioned Cushaw Pie
instructions: Preheat the oven to 204°C (200 degrees C).
Place a steamer insert into a saucepan and fill with water to just below the bottom of the steamer. Bring water to a boil. Add squash, cover, and steam until very tender, 15 to 20 minutes.
Blend squash in a blender until smooth. Pour 6 cups of squash puree into a large mixing bowl. Reserve any extra squash puree for a separate use.
Add cream, honey, eggs, molasses, cinnamon, ginger, 1 teaspoon salt, allspice, and nutmeg into the mixing bowl with squash puree; whisk until smooth. Cover and set aside.

Mix flour and 1 teaspoon salt together in a bowl. Cut butter into flour mixture; add water and mix well. Add more flour to dough if it is too wet. Cut dough in half and roll onto a floured work surface; form into two 10-inch circles. Press dough into two 10-inch pie pans; pour squash filling into each.
Bake in the preheated oven until a knife inserted in the center comes out clean, 40 to 50 minutes. Cool slightly before serving.

orange-and-hazelnut-dessert-lezzet.name: Orange And Hazelnut Dessert
instructions: 1. Extract dough into kneading container.
2. Irmic, coconut, softened butter in room heat, yogurt, powder sugar, eggs, baking powder, vanilla and reindeed orange peel.
3. pulp two parts half-century thickness hungryin oily paper.
4. Ribbed nuts with dough & pupill.
5. Wrap the dough in roll shape.
6. slice two fingers thickness.
7. Directory to oiled oven tray.
8. Öpreheated from 180 degree to fry.
9. Cook the schbet for sugar and water pot and boil for 10 minutes.
10. Add and mix orange juice and cook the pot.
11. 11. Provide the spark.
12. After washing the tray from the oven cracked after the warm harvest of strawberries u by walking to the pour.
12. August Wait until the desserts cool.
14 July Serve.

orange-blossom-caramel-sauce.name: Orange-blossom Caramel Sauce
instructions: Stir sugar, honey, salt, and 1/4 cup water in a small saucepan over medium heat until sugar dissolves. Increase heat and cook without stirring until mixture turns amber in color. Remove from heat; carefully stir in cream (mixture will bubble up), then orange-flower water. Let cool. DO AHEAD: Caramel sauce can be made 1 week ahead. Cover and chill. Rewarm slightly before using.

osobuco-with-beer-very-special-comedera.name: Osobuco With Beer, Very Special
osobuco-with-beer-very-special-comedera.original_name: Osobuco a la cerveza, muy especial
instructions: Vamos a iniciar nuestra receta, cortes los bordes de la osobuco con un cuchillo.
Procederemos, a espolvorear la harina en las piezas de la carne para sellarla.
Calentamos una cacerola con aceite de oliva.
When hot, start sealing the meat from both sides. Once ready, turn and put them off.
Mientras, iniciemos a cortar la cebolla, el puerro y filetea el ajo.
In another saucer, you're gonna heat her up with oil. Once hot, you will add garlic to them to sigh, later onion and then leek. That's a good mix.
Ahora, incorporamos a la cacerola la carne sellada, la cerveza , la sal y pimienta, la hoja de laurel. Dejamos cocinar por 10 minutos a fuego medio.
Una vez evaporado el alcohol, cubrimos con el caldo de carne o verduras y la cda de paprika, mezclamos y dejamos cocer a fuego medio durante 15 minutos.
The idea is that the flesh will cook little and little throughout the liquid, if you notice that it lacks and even the osocouple is hard, add more water and cook it till it's tender.
Deja enfriar, sirve y degusta.

osso-buco-recipe-with-orange-comedera.name: Osso Buco Recipe With Orange
osso-buco-recipe-with-orange-comedera.original_name: Receta de osobuco a la naranja

ingredients.item: 2  piezas de osobuco de ternera (aprox. 350 g cada una).
Ralladura y jugo de 2 naranjas dulces
1 taza de caldo de res (casero o bajo en sodio)
2 cdas de aceite de oliva (virgen extra )
1  ramita de tomillo fresco o 1/2 cucharadita seco
Newly ground black pepper to taste

instructions: Dry up with absorbing paper and scattering them generously from both sides.
In a large bottom pot, it heats up olive oil and dors the osocoups from both sides until they are well sealed. Take them back and put them back.
In the same pot, add onion, carrot, celery and garlic. Fold with medium heat until tender and slightly caramelized.
Incorpora el vino blanco y deja reducir durante 3-4 minutos para que se evapore el alcohol.
Add the orange juice, the frying, the balsamic vinegar, the brown sugar (if you wish to accentuate the sweetness), the re stock, the laurel and the thyme.

Return the osocoups to the pot, cover and cook with low heat for 2 hours or till the flesh is so tender that it takes off from the bone. You can also use a slow cooking pot for 6 hours at low temperature.
If you want a more thick sauce, dissolve the corn starch into a tablespoon of cold water and add it to the pot for the last 10 minutes of cooking.
Reaping the date with salt and pepper to taste.
Sirve los osobucos con su salsa y espolvorea perejil fresco por encima.

oven-baked-eggs-in-avocado-argiro.name: Oven Baked Eggs In Avocado
oven-baked-eggs-in-avocado-argiro.original_name: Ψητά αυγά στον φούρνο μέσα σε αβοκάντο
instructions: Preheat the evening to 200°C in the air.
Bake the bacon in a hot anti-stick pan for 2-3 minutes to brown.
We take it out on absorbent paper and chop it up.
Cut the ripe avocado in half and, without spoiling their shape, remove the pit. With a spoon, pour some flesh out of the avocado to make the case bigger where we cook the eggs.
We sprinkle the flesh of the avocado that we removed with a few drops of lemon so as not to oxidize and alter its green color. We're holding her aside for serving.
Place the avocado in a small baking pan just enough to hold them to be stable and do not move and empty.
Sprinkle them with some feta cheese and some crisp bacon.
Break one egg into each avocado case.
Sprinkle with salt and pepper and bake on the middle grill of the oven for about 12-14 minutes, depending on how baked we want the egg yolk.
Serve with the flesh of the avocado we kept, toasted bread and tomato salad

oven-shakshuka.name: Oven Shakshuka
instructions: Preheat the oven to 177°C (180°C).

Toss together the tomatoes, bell peppers, onion, serrano peppers, garlic, lemon juice, tomato paste, oil, harissa, salt, and 1 tablespoon of the za’atar in a 9x13-inch baking dish. Bake, uncovered, for about 30 minutes, or until most of the tomatoes have broken down and the peppers and onion have softened.

Remove from the oven. Using the back of a large spoon, create 6 evenly spaced wells in the sauce. Crack 1 egg into each well. Cover the baking dish with foil, and return to the oven. Bake for about 10 minutes, or until the egg whites are fully set and the yolks are just beginning to set but are still glossy. (For well-done but still jammy yolks, cook for an additional 15 minutes, until the yolks have set and are no longer glossy.)
Remove from the oven. Sprinkle with the feta cheese, parsley, dill, smoked paprika, and remaining 1 teaspoon za’atar. Serve immediately with warm pita bread or rustic bread, if desired.
Enjoy!

oven-roasted-chicken-gyros.name: Oven-roasted Chicken Gyros
instructions: Combine Greek yogurt, olive oil, lemon juice, vinegar, garlic, salt, paprika, black pepper, cumin, coriander, oregano, red pepper, and cinnamon in a medium bowl and stir until well combined.
Pound chicken thighs evenly to about 1/2 inch in thickness. Coat all chicken well in marinade, cover, and refrigerate at least 4 hours or overnight.

Preheat the oven to 191°C (190 degrees C). Cut ends off onion and cut onion in half horizontally. Place onion halves next to each other on a parchment or foil-lined rimmed baking sheet. Insert one skewer into each onion half, standing up vertically.
Remove chicken from marinade, allowing excess to drip off, and thread each piece of chicken over the two skewers to form a large stack.
Bake in the preheated oven until evenly browned and cooked through, about 1 hour, rotating halfway through.
Allow meat to rest for 10 minutes before slicing.
Place the sliced chicken in a warm pita topped with tzatziki sauce, lettuce, tomato, and red onion.
Serve and enjoy.

owl-cupcakes.name: Owl Cupcakes

ingredients.item: Orange or yellow candy-coated milk chocolate pieces

instructions: Preheat oven to 177°C (175 degrees C). Line 2 muffin tins with paper liners.
Combine 2 cups sugar, flour, 1 cup cocoa powder, baking powder, salt, and baking soda in a bowl.

Whisk buttermilk, vegetable oil, eggs, and vanilla extract in a large bowl. Add flour mixture; mix until well-combined. Pour in hot water; stir until batter is smooth. Divide batter evenly among muffin tins.
Bake in the preheated oven until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean, about 12 minutes. Transfer cupcakes to a wire rack and let cool completely, about 20 minutes.

Whip 3/4 cup plus 1 1/2 tablespoon heavy cream in a bowl with an electric mixer until soft peaks form. Fold in 1 1/2 cup plus 2 tablespoons mascarpone cheese, 7 tablespoons cocoa powder, and 1/4 cup sugar gently to make frosting.
Spread 1 tablespoon frosting over each cooled cupcake.

Twist chocolate sandwich cookies open, leaving all cream filling on 1 side. Place 2 cookies, cream filling-side up, on each cupcake to make owl eyes. Place a brown milk chocolate piece on each cookie to create pupils. Insert an orange or yellow milk chocolate piece in the center to make a beak.
